title: Run a Stage 2 Science Fair
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 2 Science to run a science fair that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage through real investigations.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the science from Stage 2 to run an accurate, safe and exciting science fair for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create a science-fair plan with a live investigation, labelled displays, a safety checklist, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your scientific decisions.

stages.title: 1. Foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Units 1, 2. Build a display that teaches each idea with a model or diagram, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Live investigation
stages.prompt: Pick one investigation from Units 3, 4 and run it live: state your hypothesis, follow the method, record results and explain your conclusion.
stages.evidence: A completed investigation with hypothesis, method, results and conclusion

stages.title: 3. Connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links ideas from Units 5, 6 to everyday life, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links to daily life

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your science fair. Explain at least three scientific choices, check that your conclusions match your evidence and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded scientific explanation

evidenceChecklist: A safety checklist for every activity
A connections wall with three links
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Observations, measurements, models and conclusions are accurate and checked.
Hypotheses, fair testing, recording and conclusions follow the scientific method.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the science visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate science words and evidence.

questions.question: What do ALL animals need to live?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Plants make their own food in their leaves using sunlight, water, and air. This is special to plants.
questions.unitTitle: What Do Animals and Plants Need to Live?

questions.question: What do plants use to make their own food?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: An insect has 6 legs. This is a key clue for naming a minibeast.
questions.unitTitle: What Do Animals and Plants Need to Live?

questions.question: A rock is a material that is:
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: hard and made by nature. A rock is a solid, natural material from the Earth - not made in a factory and not alive.

questions.question: The way a rock feels when you touch it is called its:
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: texture. Texture is how a surface feels: smooth, rough, bumpy, or grainy.

questions.question: When a solid gets hot and turns into a liquid, this is called:
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: melting A solid turning into a liquid when heated is called melting.

questions.question: When a liquid gets cold and turns into a solid, this is called:
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: freezing A liquid turning into a solid when cooled is called freezing.

questions.question: Which of these is a natural light source?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: the sun. The sun is made by nature, so it is a natural light source. A torch and a bulb are made by people.

questions.question: Which of these makes its OWN light?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: a candle. A candle makes its own light with its flame. The moon and a mirror only reflect light.

questions.question: Electricity is a kind of:
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: energy. Electricity is a form of energy that flows through wires.

questions.question: A complete loop that electricity flows around is called a:
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: circuit. A circuit is the complete loop electricity travels around.

questions.question: What causes day and night?
options: The Sun turns off at night
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Earth spins. Day and night are caused by Earth turning, not by the Sun switching off.

questions.question: How long does Earth take to spin around one time?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: One day (24 hours). Earth makes one full spin every 24 hours.
